program_outcomes: Matematik, fen ve mühendislik ilkelerini uygulayarak karmaşık mühendislik problemlerini tanımlama, formüle etme ve çözme becerisi.

Kamu sağlığı, güvenliği ve refahı etmenlerini ve yanı sıra küresel, kültürel, toplumsal, çevresel ve ekonomik unsurları gözetirken belirli gereksinimleri karşılayacak çözümleri üretmek için mühendislik tasarımı uygulama becerisi.
Farklı nitelikteki kitleler ile etkin bir biçimde iletişim kurma becerisi.

Mühendislik uygulamalarında mesleki ve etik sorumlulukların farkında olma ve mühendislik çözümlerinin küresel, ekonomik, çevresel ve toplumsal bağlamda etkilerini göz önünde tutan bilgiye dayalı karar verme becerisi.
Birlik içerisinde liderlik sağlayan, katılımcı ve kapsayıcı bir ortam oluşturan, amaçlar belirleyen, görevleri planlayan ve hedeflere ulaşan üyelerden oluşan bir takımda etkin işlev görme becerisi.
Uygun deney geliştirme ve yürütme, veri değerlendirme ve yorumlama ve yargı/sonuç çıkarmada mühendislik muhakeme yetisini kullanma becerisi.
Uygun öğrenme stratejileri kullanarak yeni bilgi edinme ve gerektiğinde ve gereğince uygulama becerisi.

courses.name: Physics I

courses.description: Physical quantities and measurements, unit systems; vectors; kinematic quantities and the description of motion; Newton’s laws of motion; the concepts of work and energy, conservative forces and conservation of energy; momentum and the description of the motion of particle systems, conservation of momentum; kinematics and dynamics of fixed-axis rotation; static equilibrium; harmonic motion; gravity.

To learn the concepts of scalars and vectors, their manipulations and their relation to observers/coordinate systems
To define the necessary kinematical quantities for the description of motion, and learn how to calculate them
To learn the Newton’s laws of motion and how to apply them in a coordinate system to find the kinematical quantities
To learn how to express the Newton’s laws of motion in some other ways by using quantities like work, energy, momentum
To learn how to apply Newton’s laws of motion to the rotating systems about a fixed-axis
To learn the conditions of static equilibrium
To study the fundamental properties and applications of gravity
To study small oscillations

Use key concepts related to Sustainable Development Goals.
Analyse texts using various reading strategies.
Take notes using different listening strategies.
Prepare poster presentations and give oral presentations.
Write problem solution essays on a given case.
Collaborate in group work.
Use sources accurately and effectively avoiding plagiarism.

courses.purpose: To provide the concepts and applications of the convergence of sequences and infinite series. To provide the knowledge of applications of partial differentiation and multiple integrals. To give an ability to apply knowledge of mathematics on engineering problems.

Represent a known function as a Taylor series; approximate a known function with a Taylor polynomial and determine the error involved.

courses.name: Physics II

To gain the ability of calculating electric and magnetic fields for some simple charge and current distributions. To learn how electric charges are affected by electric and magnetic fields. To understand the basic properties of electromagnetic waves using Maxwell’s equations.
courses.description: This is an introductory level physics course on electricity, magnetism and electromagnetism. The main topics are: Coulomb’s law and electrical field, Gauss’s law, electrical potential, capacitance, electrostatic energy and properties of dielectrics, current and resistance, direct current circuits, magnetic field, sources of magnetic field, Faraday’s law, inductance, Maxwell’s equations and electromagnetic waves.
learning_outcomes: To calculate the electric field and potentials for point charges and some simple continuous charge distributions
To calculate electric fields for symmetric charge distributions and properties of conductors via Gauss’ Law
To work with capacitors and the electrostatic energy stored in capacitors, understand effect of dielectric materials on capacitance and stored energy
To use Kirchhoff’s Laws and how to apply them to direct current (DC) circuits.
To calculate the interaction between magnetic fields and electric charges and current carrying wires, and the fundamental properties of magnetic fields
To calculate magnetic fields produced by moving charges and current carrying wires
To calculate the electric current/field due to time-dependent magnetic flux
To calculate the magnetic fields due to time-dependent electric fields
To work with electromagnetic waves

courses.name: Basics of Academic Writing
courses.purpose: This course aims to enhance students’ reading and writing skills simultaneously; reinforces students’ perception of the reading-writing link, enabling them to decipher academic texts more efficiently; teaches them to write academic paragraphs and argumentative essays by implementing APA in-text citation rules to avoid plagiarism.
courses.description: Reading and analyzing outside sources and eliciting proper information by differentiating general from specific data, and developing academic writing skills by including proper in-text citation (direct quotation and paraphrase) based on APA citation rules, critical thinking, planning, drafting and editing, interpreting charts and graphs, participating in in-class discussions of the reading materials.
learning_outcomes: Students who pass the course satisfactorily can identify the main points, analyze the view and the tone of the text, and distinguish the general information from the specific one.
Students who pass the course satisfactorily can elicit relevant information from an outside source to support their ideas.

Students who pass the course satisfactorily can follow the academic writing rules and support their ideas in their writing by referring to an outside source using direct quoting, paraphrasing, and summarising methods and applying APA in-text citation rules.
Students who pass the course satisfactorily can identify unity and coherence in given texts and apply them in their written or oral products.
Students who pass the course satisfactorily can use the relevant vocabulary and structures reflecting different opinions in their written and oral products.
Students who pass the course satisfactorily can avoid plagiarism and meet the requirements of academic integrity in their oral presentations and written products.
Students who pass the course satisfactorily can interpret and use figures and tables from academic sources in their oral presentations and written products using appropriate terminology.

courses.name: Entrepreneurship and Career Advising
courses.purpose: This course aims to enable undergraduate students to develop a professional vision, gain the skills needed to achieve their personal career goals, communicate one-on-one with various role models, and thereby enhance their entrepreneurial abilities.
courses.description: This course introduces undergraduate students to entrepreneurship and career opportunities both within their program and in broader fields. It presents the relationship between career planning and entrepreneurship through expert academicians and invited speakers from industry and business sectors.
learning_outcomes: Students will be able to make career plans.
Students will understand the basic concepts of entrepreneurship.
Students can be placed in jobs suitable for their own characteristics after graduation.
Students will be able to adapt to working life after graduation.
Students will be able to communicate with role model individuals in person from their national and international colleagues.
Students will gain experience from the experiences of graduates.

To provide students with theoretical and practical knowledge to analyse the data structure needs of particular problems. The student will learn the principles, applications, and implementations of dynamic data structures.
Students will be able to solve problems using advanced programming concepts like pointers and recursion.
The student will learn to design, implement, test, and debug computer programs using C++ for engineering applications.

courses.name: Digital Circuits
courses.purpose: To teach how integers are represented in computers using the binary system, how Boolean algebra is used in digital logic, and how digital circuits are designed with cost criteria in mind. The course also introduces latches and flip-flops and covers the analysis and design of synchronous sequential circuits.
courses.description: Representation of integers in binary system, Boolean algebra, combinational logic circuit design, synchronous sequential circuit analysis and synthesis.
learning_outcomes: Ability to perform arithmetic operations on binary integers and to interpret the results.
Ability to manipulate and simplify logic expressions using the axioms and theorems of the Boolean algebra.
Ability to design logic functions using only universal gates (i.e. NAND and NOR).
Ability to find the prime implicants of logic functions using Karnaugh map and tabular (Quine-McCluskey) methods, and implement these functions with minimum cost.
Ability to design digital systems using MSI building blocks (Adders, multiplexers, decoders).
Knowing functional and timing (clocking) properties of basic memory units (latches and flip-flops).
Ability to analyze synchronous sequential circuits.
Ability to design synchronous sequential circuits.
Knowing the internal structure of basic CMOS circuits.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction: Characteristics of digital systems, representation of integers in digital systems, arithmetical operations on binary integers
Hafta 2: Fundamentals of Boolean Algebra, axioms and theorems
Hafta 3: Representation of logic functions, standard (canonical) forms
Hafta 4: Design of logic functions using only universal gates (i.e. NAND and NOR)
Hafta 5: Finding prime implicants using Karnaugh maps. Finding essential and sufficient prime implicants.
Hafta 6: Finding prime implicants using Tabular (Quine-McCluskey) method. Finding prime implicants for the functions in the sum-of-products form
Hafta 7: MSI building blocks (adders, multiplexers). Design of combinational logic circuits using adders and multiplexers.
Hafta 8: Decoders, Programmable Logic devices (PAL, PLA). Design of combinational logic circuits using these devices.
Hafta 9: Latches, flip-flops, registers
Hafta 10: Analysis of synchronous sequential circuits (Mealy model)
Hafta 11: Analysis of synchronous sequential circuits (Moore model)
Hafta 12: Design of synchronous sequential circuits
Hafta 13: Design of counters as a sequential circuit in Moore model
Hafta 14: Electrical characteristics of logic circuits. Internal structures of CMOS circuits

courses.purpose: Understanding the behavior of semi-conductor electronic devices and their terminal characteristics. Learning how to bias these devices. Introduction to analog and digital circuits employing these devices.

Understand their terminal characteristics
Know how to bias these devices
Can analyze and design basic electronic circuits employing these devices

courses.description: Writing an academic paper comprising the following research process: choosing a topic of interest related to the field of study, researching, selecting sources, developing and defending a specific viewpoint based on reliable sources, critical thinking, outlining, citing sources according to APA standards, and preparing a references page.
learning_outcomes: Conduct extensive library and internet research to select suitable, relevant, and reliable scholarly sources related to a chosen topic.
Identify different kinds of sources and logically organize findings from a variety of sources in the form of an outline.
Document source information by correctly applying APA style citations and references.
Locate, evaluate, interpret, and synthesize source information for the defense of the thesis.
Perform extensive note taking with the correct application of paraphrases, summaries, and quotations.
Draw personal conclusions, make deductions, and form comments based on the research findings.
Prepare and present an expository or analytical paper in APA style.
Adhere to the norms of research ethics.

courses.name: Computer Organization
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce the fundamental digital structures used in computer design and to develop the ability to design an elementary computer system. It also covers control unit design, including hardware and microprogramming approaches, and algorithmic state machines for sequencing and control.
courses.description: Design of central processing unit, arithmetic logic unit, control unit, I/O operations using interrupts, memory and bus design.
learning_outcomes: Design of central processing unit
Design of arithmetic logic unit
Design of hardware and microprogramming based control unit
I/O operations using interrupts
Memory and bus design
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Digital Components: Decoders, Multiplexers, Shift registers
Hafta 2: Binary Counters, Memory Unit. Microoperations: Register Transfer Language, Register Transfer, Bus Transfer
Hafta 3: Memory Transfer, Arithmetic Microoperations, Logic Microoperations, Shift Microoperations
Hafta 4: Arithmetic Lojic Shift Unit. Basic Computer Organization and Design: Instruction Codes, Computer Registers
Hafta 5: Computer Instructions, Timing and Control, Instruction Cycle
Hafta 6: Memory Reference Instructions, Input / Output and Interrupt. Complete Computer Description
Hafta 7: Design of Basic Computer. Microprogrammed Control: Control Memory, Address Sequencing
Hafta 8: Microprogram Example, Design of Control Unit
Hafta 9: Class Work
Hafta 10: Stack Organization. Control Unit, Algorithmic State Machines, Design Example 1: Binary Multiplier
Hafta 11: Hardwired Control, Microprogrammed Control
Hafta 12: Design Example 2: Traffic Light Controller. Design Example 3: Data Processor.
Hafta 13: Design Example 3: Multi-core processor
Hafta 14: Design Example 4: 1 clock cycle processor
resources: M. Morris Mano, Computer System Architecture, 3/e, Prentice Hall, 1993
William Stallings, Computer Organization & Architecture, 6/e, Prentice Hall, 2003
Andrew S. Tanenbaum, Structured Computer Organization, 4/e, Prentice Hall, 1999

courses.name: Logic Circuits Laboratory
courses.purpose: The laboratory will cover practical topics that extend concepts learned in class. Students will be able to implement and debug both combinational and sequential circuits. They will also be able to write reports.
courses.description: A laboratory involving the design and implementation of logic circuits. Combinational and sequential (both synchronous and asynchronous) design examples using small and medium scale integrated circuits. PAL/PLA programming software and HDL (Verilog) is also used. Groups of 3 students use a protoboard to build their circuits.
learning_outcomes: Demonstrate basic laboratory skills, including the use of standard laboratory equipment.
Design and implement logic circuits (combinational and synchronous sequential) using small-scale and medium-scale integrated circuits.
Test and debug the logic circuits.
Use computer software to program programmable logical devices.
Prepare a written report that effectively communicates the objective, the design procedure, the experimental results, and the conclusion for any project design.
To work with group members in harmony to solve problems
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to lab. equipment and forming the groups.
Hafta 2: Experiment 0: Familiarization
Hafta 3: Experiment 1: Boolean Algebra
Hafta 4: Submission of the report of the previous experiment. Experiment 2: Implementation of the combinational circuits
Hafta 5: Submission of the report of the previous experiment. Experiment 3: Binary Numbers and Arithmetic Operations
Hafta 6: Submission of the report of the previous experiment. Experiment 4: Design of Combinational Logic Circuits by Using the Programmable Logic Devices
Hafta 7: Submission of the report of the previous experiment. Review.
Hafta 8: Experiment 5: Electrical Characteristics of Logical Gates
Hafta 9: Submission of the report of the previous experiment. Experiment 6: Using the Common bus in digital systems
Hafta 10: Submission of the report of the previous experiment. Experiment 7: Latch and flipflops
Hafta 11: Submission of the report of the previous experiment. Experiment 8: Design of sequential circuits
Hafta 12: Submission of the report of the previous experiment. Make up experiment
Hafta 13: Submission of the report of the previous experiment. Make up experiment
Hafta 14: Review.
resources: Laboratory Handouts
M. Mano, M. Ciletti, Digital Design, 6/e, Pearson, 2019

courses.name: Turkish I
courses.purpose: To improve the consciousness of national language, to provide knowledge about the history, structure and characteristics of Turkish Language, to develop the ability to use language properly and effectively in both spoken and written forms, and to give information about Turkish Poetry.
courses.description: Definition of language, language and mind, language and culture, world languages in terms of origin and structure, the significance of Turkish among world languages, the historical development of Turkish, the structure of Turkish, Turkish phonetics, modern Turkish language, the act of writing and orthography, spelling rules, correct expression, scientific language and Turkish as a science language, Turkish poetry and poetry language.
learning_outcomes: Understand the definition of language with respect to intellectual, cultural and social aspects.
Understand the situation of Turkish Language among the world languages.
Have information about the structure, characteristics and history of Turkish Language.
Make effective use of both the spoken and written languages.
Have knowledge about poetry language and Turkish Poetry.

courses.name: Principles of Computer Comm.
courses.purpose: To teach the basic concepts of computer communications, analyze computer networking using the Shannon diagram, examine the layered structure of computer communications in detail, and introduce the principles of the physical and data link layers including wired and wireless technologies.
courses.description: Introduction to computer communications and basic communications concepts. Shannon diagram, communication principle using source-channel-destination nodes. Data communications and entropy. Introduction to International Standards Organization Open SystemInterconnection (ISO-OSI) reference model, design issues and protocols in the physical layer, data link layer and network layer, architectures and control algorithms, standards in network access protocols and models of network interconnection.
learning_outcomes: Learn the principles of computer communications
Understand the basics of layered architecture of computer networks
Learn the physical and data link layers in computer networks.
Learn the current wired and wireless technologies.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction. Basics of communications, the evolution of communication concepts
Hafta 2: Shannon diagram, channel basics, Representation of data and Information.
Hafta 3: Layered architecture in computer networks, physical layer principles.
Hafta 4: Physical layer concepts in wired networks, modulation and multiplexing
Hafta 5: Spectral analysis in physical layer. Electromagnetic spectrum, fading and interference.
Hafta 6: Data link layer principles,framing
Hafta 7: Error detection and correction. LANs.
Hafta 8: Bridging in wired and wireless topologies, spanning tree bridges, switches, virtual LANs.
Hafta 9: Medium Acces Control (MAC) taxonomy and protocols.
Hafta 10: Sliding window protocols, congestion and flow controls
Hafta 11: Wireless LAN,cellular networks,
Hafta 12: cell capacity planning
Hafta 13: 2G,3G and 4G technologies, current wired and wireless computer communications
Hafta 14: Network standardization
resources: Computer networking, a top-down approach featuring the Internet’, James F.Kurose, Keith W.Ross, Pearson-Addison Wesley, 7th edition, 2017.
Data Communications and Networking, Behrouz A. Forouzan, McGraw Hill, 5th edition, 2013.

courses.name: Logic & Computability
courses.purpose: To teach inference in common logical models, inference machines, the limits of computability, generalized computable system design, and novel computation proposals.
courses.description: Foundations of computability, inference and proof in common logical models, explanation of inference machines, limits of computation machines, classification and measurement of computation, computation of generalized problems, computability of learning, future of computability.
learning_outcomes: Knowing the foundations of computability
Being able to infer in logical models.
Recognizing the machines that could infer
Being able to classify computation from several perspectives.
Knowing the generalized computational models

courses.name: Operations Research
courses.purpose: The course aims to teach the methods used in quantitative decision making, to formulate real-life business problems through modeling, and to interpret the obtained results from technical and economic viewpoints.
courses.description: Quantitative decision making; model building and modeling philosophy; linear programming and the Simplex algorithm; Big M method; two-phase Simplex method; duality and sensitivity analysis; transportation models and solution methods; integer programming; network models; LINDO, LINGO, and GAMS software packages.
learning_outcomes: Formulate the problems occurring in different kinds of systems
Collect necessary data to determine the parameters affecting the problems dealt with
Develop mathematical models that represent the problems dealt with ideally
Test the developed mathematical models about their convenience with the real systems
Solve the developed models (Linear Programming, Transportation, Assignment) and find the best solution for the problems dealt with
Use different software to solve the developed models (Linear Programming, Transportation, Assignment)

courses.name: Tech.&Innov. Mng.for Inf.Tech.
courses.purpose: To develop an awareness of the range, scope, and complexity of the issues and problems related to the strategic management of technology and innovation. To develop an understanding of the state of the art methods for the strategic management of technology and innovation. To introduce a conceptual framework for assessing and auditing the innovative capabilities of a business organization in the field of information technologies.
courses.description: This course will teach the importance of an innovative and creative view of information technology. It will cover how organizations can commercialize their technological innovations and how the associated risks and benefits might be managed. Assembling internal and external ideas through the open innovation paradigm and transferring innovations inward and outward will be discussed.
learning_outcomes: Ability to work in the area of business development, strategic projects or innovation management in a national or international IT company
Knowledge on information technology and innovation in business terms
The understanding that the basis for business opportunity is change resulting from technology and the innovations
A mind set as well as a tool set to enable them to correctly select the models required to provide value to their organizations
Ability to interact with and discuss cases with their team members in their prospective organizations in order to present and defend their arguments
Ability to use both team experience and individual efforts to master the fundamentals of management of technology and innovation

Hafta 2: Empirical findings: success factors

courses.name: Materials Science
courses.purpose: To give an understanding on the importance of materials science for engineering applications. To teach the properties, structure and production processes of materials and to make the students understand the strong relations between them. To give an understanding on the material selection and design using material science knowledge.
courses.description: Introduction to engineering materials, atomic structure and bonding; crystalline materials, crystal structures, crystal imperfections and atomic movements; mechanical properties of materials including tensile testing, elastic and plastic deformation, fracture, wear, fatigue, creep and hardness; phase diagrams and solidification; strengthening mechanisms; annealing, phase transformations and heat treatment; ferrous and non-ferrous alloys; ceramic, polymer and composite materials; electrical, dielectric, thermal, semiconducting, optical, piezoelectric and magnetic properties of materials; corrosion and prevention.
learning_outcomes: Understand the relations between the chemical composition, atomic bonding and the properties of materials
Understand the ordered and disordered crystal structures and the differences between them and define Bravais lattices and Miller indices
Understand the effects of crystal defects on material properties and the test methods for the determination of mechanical properties.
Understand the diffusion concept and laws and its effects on material properties.
Understand the importance of phase concept and phase diagrams, and use them
Classify ferrous and non-ferrous alloy systems and understand the properties of polymeric and ceramic materials.
Understand the strengthening mechanisms of materials, phase transformations and heat treatments
Understand electronic and electrical, thermal and magnetic properties of materials and interrelate them with the structure
Understand the importance of corrosion and prevention methods

Hafta 2: Crystalline materials, crystal structures, crystal imperfection and atomic movements in materials
Hafta 3: Mechanical properties of materials: tensile test, elastic, plastic deformation, fracture
Hafta 4: Mechanical properties of materials: wear, fatigue, creep and hardness
Hafta 5: Phase diagrams
Hafta 6: Phase diagrams and solidification
Hafta 7: Strengthening mechanisms: grain size, solid solution, dispersion, deformation strengthening

identify the structure of microprocessor-based systems.

courses.name: Mythology and Art
courses.purpose: To provide knowledge about the importance of mythology as a remarkable source of inspiration, and its role in the formation of culture and civilization. To provide a rich and multi-perspectival point of view to art, history and culture, and to cultivate designing creativity through a broad aesthetic sensibility.
courses.description: The birth of myths; the sources of Greek and Roman mythology; formation of cosmos in Greek mythology; the fundamental personalities and their myths in Greek mythology; influence of Greek myths on the myths of other cultures; mythological depictions in Ancient Greek, Hellenistic and Roman arts; stylistic and conceptual differences in mythological depictions in the artistic periods and art movements like the Renaissance, Mannerism, Baroque, Rococo, Neo-Classical, Romanticism, Pre-Raphaelite and Symbolism; mythological depictions in modern and post-modern art.
learning_outcomes: Have profound knowledge about Greek and Roman mythology and their influences on cultural history.
Attain the ability to identify fundamental eras, styles and artists in the history of Western Art.
Have knowledge about Art History and History of Civilization through artworks while learning more on their nation’s artistic and cultural differences between other nations.
Achieve a profound artistic and esthetical sensibility which they can make use of in the process of professional creativity and designing.
Gain information on museums and experience of a direct contact with artworks through museum visits.

Hafta 5: The fundamental personalities and myths in Greek mythology 4 - İliad and Odyssey by Homeros, figures and stories from these two legends

courses.name: Traditional Turkish Art&Crafts
courses.purpose: To inform students about the richness of Turkish culture and the basic elements that gave rise to arts and crafts, to provide foundational knowledge about the technical development, materials, and production processes of traditional Turkish art and crafts, and to raise awareness of the relationship between local Turkish culture, global art, and contemporary art practice.
courses.description: General information and visual analyses on the major traditional art and crafts of Turkish art: Art of Books (binding, calligraphy, illumination, painting, filigree, marbled paper), Tiles and Ceramics, Carpets, Textiles, Metalwork, Woodwork, and Stonework. The course also covers the current practice of traditional arts and crafts.
learning_outcomes: Students who pass the course will be able to learn the basics of traditional Turkish art and crafts.
Learn the topics and motifs used in traditional Turkish artworks.
Gain information on the richness of Turkish art and culture.
Recognize museums and get acquainted with the artworks exhibited there.
Learn about the production techniques of traditional artworks and the engineering methods of that era.
Gain an aesthetic perspective over artworks and their decorative elements.

courses.name: Ancient Civilizat.in Anatolia

be aware of the importance of protection of the cultural assets
recognize the museums; get acquainted with the exhibited artworks and material culture objects
gain an aesthetic perspective over the ancient artworks
determine the existing traditions of today’s culture and their relationship between cultural history
gain knowledge to use the information as an entry for their professional work

courses.name: Theater

courses.name: Film Production
courses.purpose: To provide students with a basic understanding of filmmaking and to encourage them to use films as a source of inspiration for their professional development. The course also aims to broaden students' visual perception and help them become active, critical viewers while introducing the practical knowledge needed for filmmaking.
courses.description: In this course students will investigate the techniques of short film production. Basic film terminology, basic camera setups, sound recording, understanding the roles of the various members of the production team and mastering the video camera as a means of communication are among the topics of this course. This semester course will culminate in an exhibition of original student video projects.
learning_outcomes: Learn about the filmmaking process and gain a certain comfort level in using basic film equipment.

courses.name: Art, Culture and Society
courses.purpose: This lecture aims to inform engineering students about art history as it develops together with the history of civilization and culture, to show how art is shaped by political, social, economic, cultural, scientific, and technological developments, and to help students develop a holistic awareness of art. It also seeks to strengthen analytical thinking and critical analysis in relation to art, society, and culture, while encouraging engagement with museums, galleries, and exhibitions.
courses.description: This lecture examines the material culture of mankind from antiquity to the present in relation to aesthetic needs, observes the artistic contributions of culture and society, and studies how intercultural communication and interaction shape art practice. It also discusses the effects of political, social, economic, scientific, and technological developments on art.
learning_outcomes: Gain the skill to understand the relation between art, society and culture while evaluating artworks.
Learn the formation of artistic production based on sociological changes and interactions within the history of civilization.
Develop the skill of objective intellectual view on the world art culture through samples.
Learn more on the city they live in and develop theoretical visual information through the field trips to art sites and various artistic activities.

courses.name: Philosophy of Art
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with a deeper understanding of the philosophy of art and to develop their critical and analytical thinking about art-related issues. It also seeks to strengthen theoretical and conceptual thinking, communication, discussion, reading, research, and writing skills.
courses.description: This course is intended to be a general introduction to the major theories, concepts and issues encountered in the Philosophy of Art. It will critically examine, compare, contrast and assess philosophical arguments within the context of various essays written by various philosophers. The course addresses questions such as what art is, what defines an artwork, what aesthetic qualities are, the meanings of aesthetic taste, attitude and experience, whether aesthetic judgments are merely subjective expressions, and whether there is any relationship between reality and representation in art.
learning_outcomes: Understand and employ key aesthetic theories and concepts
Think theoretically and conceptually
Communicate and discuss issues effectively
Read and understand texts, make research and write
Locate and evaluate engineering problems in their social contexts

courses.name: Computer Operating Systems
courses.purpose: Teach students fundamentals of operating systems, design issues, algorithms and structures. Programming projects aim to provide experience to support basic concepts.
courses.description: Introduction, history. Processes: basic concepts, concurrent processes, mutual exclusion, process management, scheduling approaches. Deadlock and deadlock prevention approaches. Memory management: segmentation, paging, related methods, virtual memory. Input/Output. UNIX and other example operating systems.
learning_outcomes: Students will learn the basic concepts of process management.
Students will learn techniques for interprocess communication and synchronization. When given a problem involving concurrent processes, they will be able to design and code a solution to the problem.
Students will learn a range of algorithms for process scheduling and deadlock detection and avoidance.
Students will learn concepts of memory management (allocation, paging, segmentation, virtual memory).
Students will learn file management and input/output handling in operating systems.
Students will learn and be able to apply Unix system calls.
Students will be able to use operating systems features to solve real world problems.

courses.description: This non-lecture course contains a design project. Two or three-person groups can be formed for the project. The project may be a software or hardware project and covers the process from project selection to preliminary project analysis studies. The course enables students to use the knowledge they have gained during their education in the department.
learning_outcomes: An ability to analyse a system, component, or process to meet desired needs.
An ability to identify engineering problems.
An ability to learn about the techniques, skills, and modern engineering tools necessary for engineering practice.
An ability to communicate effectively.
An ability to produce a detailed project analysis report.

courses.name: Programming in Parallel and Distributed Systems
Object Oriented Modeling and Design
courses.purpose: To teach the importance of following a process driven by stakeholder requirements, object-oriented design principles and the concepts behind them, how to design flexible and reusable software components, and how to use design patterns to solve real-world software design problems.
courses.description: Principles and patterns of object-oriented design that are used to create flexible and reusable software modules. This course also covers some basic topics of object-oriented software development, such as unified process (UP), use cases, and domain modeling. Analysis and design models will be presented using the UML.
learning_outcomes: Knowing the problems in the software industry and being aware of the need for a high-quality design
Ability to perform requirement analysis and write use cases
Ability to analyze the problem domain and create the domain model
Knowing important object-oriented design principles such as “expert”, “low coupling”, “high cohesion”, “indirection”, and “polymorphism”
Knowing the reasoning for object-oriented design principles and patterns, such as flexibility, reusability, and understandability
Knowing important GoF design patterns
Ability to use design patterns to solve real-world software design problems
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction. Problems in the software industry, main topics of the course
Hafta 2: Requirement Analysis, Use Cases
Hafta 3: Analysis, domain modeling
Hafta 4: Larman (GRASP) Design Principles: Controller, Creator, Expert, Low coupling, High cohesion
Hafta 5: Design examples using Larman (GRASP) Design Principles
Hafta 6: Larman (GRASP) Design Principles: Polymorphism, Pure Fabrication, Indirection, Protected Variations
Hafta 7: Design examples using Larman (GRASP) Design Principles
Hafta 8: Introduction to GoF design patterns. Adapter, Factory, Singleton
Hafta 9: GoF design patterns. Strategy, Composite
Hafta 10: GoF design patterns. Facade, pluggable design rules, Observer
Hafta 11: Design examples using GoF design patterns
Hafta 12: GoF design patterns. Decorator, Template method
Hafta 13: GoF design patterns. Iterator, Bridge
Hafta 14: Design examples using GoF design patterns
resources: Craig Larman, Applying UML and Patterns, An Introduction to OOA/D and Iterative Development, 3/e, Prentice Hall PTR, 2005.
Eric & Elisabeth Freeman: Head First Design Patterns, 2/e, O’REILLY, 2020.
Gamma E., Helm R., Johnson R., Vlissides J., Design Patterns: Elements of Reusable Object-Oriented Software, Reading MA, Addison-Wesley, 1995.

courses.name: Robotics
courses.purpose: To provide information on robots, their components and applications. To give an ability to design the main control architecture, localization, mapping, path planning and vision algorithms of a robot system. To give an ability to design cognitive robot skills.
courses.description: Introduces different types, components and applications of robots, mechanical designs and different sensors. Presents control architectures of robots, localization, mapping, path planning and robot vision algorithms. Introduces cognitive robot skills such as planning, knowledge representation and learning. Presents advanced subjects, such as multirobot systems, and human-robot interaction.
learning_outcomes: Have a broad perspective in robot types, components and applications.
Determine the most appropriate robot type for a real-world problem.
Solve kinematic calculations of mobile robots.
Determine and implement the main control architecture of a robot system.
Determine and implement the most appropriate localization, mapping, path planning and vision methods.
Add high level planning and learning capabilities to robots, and equip robots with cognitive skills.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Robots and their applications, types of robots
Hafta 2: Robot Control Architectures
Hafta 3: Robot Mechanics and Locomotion
Hafta 4: Robot Operating System (ROS)
Hafta 5: Mobile Robot Kinematics
Hafta 6: Robot Sensors
Hafta 7: Localization
Hafta 8: Mapping
Hafta 9: Path Planning
Hafta 10: Robot Vision
Hafta 11: Robot Learning
Hafta 12: Robot Manipulation
Hafta 13: Multirobot Systems and Human-robot interaction
Hafta 14: Robot Applications and Project Presentations
resources: Introduction to Autonomous Mobile Robots, Roland Siegwart, Illah Reza Nourbakhsh and Davide Scaramuzza, The MIT Press, 2011
Springer Handbook of Robotics, Bruno Siciliano and Oussama Khatib, Springer, 2016
Probabilistic Robotics, Sebastian Thrun, Wolfram Burgard and Dieter Fox, MIT Press, 2005
Developmental robotics: From babies to robots, Angelo Cangelosi, Matthew Schlesinger, MIT Press, 2015
Computational Principles of Mobile Robotics, Gregory Dudek and Michael Jenkin, Cambridge University Press, 2nd edition, 2010

courses.name: German I
courses.purpose: This course aims to enable students to introduce themselves with simple sentences, talk about their hobbies and preferences, express themselves in daily life, recognize country names, cities, famous places and the languages spoken in those countries, and use basic shopping terms.
courses.description: It includes teaching basic German grammar and vocabulary (Wortschatz) and preparing students for the Goethe certificate exams at the A1 level, which are organized within the framework of the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages.
learning_outcomes: Creates a text of at least 50 words using simple phrases and sentences to describe himself, the place he lives and the people he knows.
Can write an invitation letter with short and simple sentences.
Can understand familiar names, words and very simple sentences in written texts such as catalogues, announcements or posters.
Can introduce someone, offer simple greetings and farewells.
He can shop in a simple way when he can explain what he wants by showing or using gestures and facial expressions. (Numbers, quantities, prices)
Can ask others questions about themselves.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: -Kurun Tanıtımı: (yöntem,malzeme) -Almancanın bugüne kadar geçirdiği evreler. -Kendini tanıtmak

Hafta 2: Start auf Deutsch -İngilizce ile ortak kullanılan kelimeler üzerinden basit metinler okumak -1’den 10’a kadar sayılar. Belirli ve belirsiz Artikel’lere giriş. -Küçük dialoglar . Sein “olmak” fiili
Hafta 3: -Tüm şahıslarda düzenli fiiller. -Yiyecek ve içecek malzemelerinin Almanca karşılıkları - Konuyla ilgili kelimeler ve belirtme Artikel’lerin pekiştirilmesi.
Hafta 4: - Düzensiz sıfatlar. -Kafeteryada hesap istemek -1den 100e kadar olan sayılar -Sözlü anlama (kendini tanıtma eylemi).
Hafta 5: -Sınıf içerisinde derste kullanılan kalıplar -Ders ve okul malzemelerinin Almanca karşılıkları - Konuyla ilgili kelimeler ve belirtme Artikel’lerin pekiştirilmesi.
Hafta 6: -Ülke, uyruk ve dil belirtmek -Haritada yön belirtmek - Konuyla ilgili kelimeler ve belirtme Artikel’lerin pekiştirilmesi.
Hafta 7: ARA SINAV
Hafta 8: -Öğrencilerin kendilerini ve ülkelerini tanıtan kısa metinler oluşturmaları -Grafik değerlendirmek

Hafta 9: -Sözlü anlam ve alıştırmalar -Olumsuzluk üzerine alıştırmalar. (nicht- oder kein) -Sıfat tamlamalarının açıklanması ve kullanılması -İsim tamlamasının ( açıklanması ve kullanımı -Belirli ve belirsiz Artikel’lerin kullanımı ile ilgili çalışmalar -Ödev kontrolü

Hafta 10: -Almanya, Avusturya ve İsviçre’deki yaşama tarzları, ev biçimleri -Yaşadığın şehri ve oturduğun evi betimleme -Konuyla ilgili sıaftların öğrenilmesi - Konuyla ilgili kelimeler ve belirtme Artikel’lerin pekiştirilmesi.
Hafta 11: -İyelik zamirleri (Personalpronomen) -İsmin halleri (AKKUSATIV) -Evde kullanılan eşyaların Almanca karşılıkları - Konuyla ilgili kelimeler ve belirtme Artikel’lerin pekiştirilmesi.

Hafta 12: -Gün, ay ve tarih belirtme -Saat belirtme -Randevulaşma - Konuyla ilgili nesneleri belirtme Artikel’lerin pekiştirilmesi. -Ayrılabilen fiillerin kullanımı (Trennbare Verben) -Soru edatlarının kullanımı (Fragewörter)
Hafta 13: -Yönler ve yol tarifleri -Yön bulma, plan okuma, yol sorma -Sıra sayıları -İşaret sıfatları -İyelik sıfatları(Possessivpronomen)
Hafta 14: FİNAL SINAVI

courses.name: German II
courses.purpose: Enabling students to express themselves using the past tense (Präteritum), ask for and give directions, talk about daily activities, use the imperative form, and prepare for the Goethe A1 exam.
courses.description: It includes teaching basic German grammar and vocabulary and the second level of preparatory work for the Goethe certificate exams at the A1 level, organized within the framework of the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages.
learning_outcomes: Can understand what he/she is listening to when spoken very slowly and distinctly and when long intervals are given to allow him/her to grasp the meaning.
Can understand questions and instructions and understand short, simple messages when approached very slowly and carefully.
Can tell what time it is.
Can understand information about people in newspaper texts.
Can understand a form in a way that gives the most important information about it.
Can give detailed directions and briefly describe his/her place of residence.
Can write short postcards and letters.
Can give more detailed information about himself and his family.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: A1.1 kurunda öğrenilen bilgilerin hatırlatılması ve tekrar çalışmaları

Hafta 2: Meslekler; mesleklerin Almanca karşılıkları ve görev tanımları; konuyla ilgili kelimeler ve belirtme Artikel’lerin pekiştirilmesi; Dativ alan edatlar (Praepositionen mit Dativ); sıra sayıları; işaret sıfatları
Hafta 3: Hayalindeki mesleği tanıtma; yardımcı filler (Modalverben- müssen und können); iyelik zamirlerinin Akkusativ olarak çekimlenmesi
Hafta 4: Berlin şehrinin tanıtılması; yönler ve yol tarifleri
Hafta 5: Yön bulma, plan okuma, yol sorma; Akkusativ ile kullanılan edatlar (in, durch, über etc.)
Hafta 6: Yardımcı filler (Modalverben- wollen); Almanya’daki tatil yerlerinin tanıtılması; tatil ile ilgili kelimelerin öğrenilmesi; geçmiş zaman (Perfekt) konusuna giriş
Hafta 7: ARA SINAV
Hafta 8: Geçmiş zamanın soru cümlelerinde kullanımı; geçmiş zamanın olumsuz kullanımı; geçmiş zaman zaman belirteçleri; geçmiş olaylardan bahsetmek

Hafta 9: Yiyecek ve içeceklerin Almanca karşılıklarının öğrenilmesi; alışverişte kullanılan terimler; yemek tarifinin anlaşılması; yemek tarifi oluşturulması; sıfatlarda kıyaslama (Komparation); soru edatlarının ismin hallerine göre çekimlenmesi
Hafta 10: Kıyafetler; kıyafetlerle ilgili yeni kelimelerin öğrenilmesi; hava durumu
Hafta 11: Renkler; sıfat tamlamaları (Adjaktivdeklinationen); “Es” sözcüğünün özne olarak kullanıldığı yerler
Hafta 12: Vücut organlarının Almanca karşılıklarını öğrenme; sağlıkla ilgili metinler okuma; ödev kontrolü
Hafta 13: Emir kipleri (Imperativ); yardımcı filler (Modalverben- dürfen); Personalpronomen im Akkusativ
Hafta 14: FİNAL SINAVI
resources: “MENSCHEN A.1.2” HUEBER VERLAG BERLİN (2012)
Billinca A., ReIMANN M.(2012)“ ÜBUNGSGRAMMATIK FÜR DIE GRUNDSTUFE“ HUEBER VERLAG
LEMCKE C., ROHRMANN L. (2006)”GRAMMATIK INTENSIVTRAINER A1” LANGENSCHEIDT VERLAG
HÖRTEXTE UND EINFACHE LESESTÜCKE

courses.name: German III
courses.purpose: This course aims to enable students to describe past events, make physical and character descriptions, use quantity expressions, time adverbs and prepositions, express the flow of time, use personal pronouns in sentences, and talk about feelings and wishes using the subjunctive conditional mood.
courses.description: It includes teaching intermediate-level German grammar and vocabulary (Wortschatz), as well as preparation for the A2 level of the Goethe certificate exams, which are organized within the framework of the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages (CEFR).
learning_outcomes: Can write simple sentences; They can use conjunctions such as “and,” “but,” “because,” “before,” “then,” “later,” and “lastly.”
They can generally understand the subject when spoken to around them slowly and clearly.
They can describe a past event in a short and simple way.
They can write a composition of 100-120 words.
They can talk about their leisure activities.
They can make sentences expressing cause and effect.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: A1.2 kurunda öğrenilen bilgilerin hatırlatılması ve tekrar çalışmaları
Hafta 2: Çokdillilik kavramı ve diller üzerinde konuşma çalışmaları; Geçmiş zaman (Praeteritum) kullanımı ile ilgili çalışmalar
Hafta 3: Geçmiş zaman edatının kullanımı (Nebensazetze mit als)
Hafta 4: Ailenin ve aile bireylerinin tanıtımı ile ilgili çalışmalar; Bu konu bağlamında okuma-anlama becerisini geliştirmek için metin üzerinden yapılan soru ve cevaplar; Kelime bilgisi çalışmaları
Hafta 5: Geçmiş zamanlardan Praeteritum ve Partizip Perfekt'in farklılıkları ve bir arada kullanım kuralları ve bu kullanımlar ile ilgili açıklayıcı çalışmalar
Hafta 6: Ünite ile ilgili diyalog okuma-anlama; Kelime bilgisi çalışmaları; Seyahat ve tatil konuları ile ilgili kelimelerin öğrenilmesi; Konu ile ilgili okuma çalışmaları
Hafta 7: Ara sınav
Hafta 8: Sebep-sonuç ilişkisi bildiren bağlaçların kullanımı (denn-weil); Gelecek zamanın kullanımı (werden)
Hafta 9: Hobi ve boş zaman faaliyetleri ile ilgili kelimelerin öğrenilmesi; Konu ile ilgili kelimeleri pekiştirmek amaçlı diyalog dinlemesi
Hafta 10: Dativ alan fiiller ve ilgi edatları (Verben und Praepositionen mit Dativ); Dilek şart kipinin kullanımının (Wenn Saetze) pekiştirilmesi; Ödev kontrol
Hafta 11: Kitle iletişim araçları ile ilgili kelimelerin öğrenilmesi; Relativsaetze mit +in
Hafta 12: Kitle iletişim araçları ile ilgili metin okuma çalışması
Hafta 13: Boş zaman faaliyetleri ile ilgili çalışmalar
Hafta 14: Final sınavı
resources: MENSCHEN A.2.1, Hueber Verlag Berlin (2012)
Billinca A., Reimann M. (2012), Übunggrammatik für die Grundstufe, Hueber Verlag
C. Rohrmann L. (2006), Grammatik Intensivtrainer A-2, Langenscheidt Verlag
Hörtexte und einfache Lesestücke

courses.name: German IV
courses.purpose: To enable students to talk about projects using the future tense, use quantity and place pronouns, describe a house they may buy or rent, understand real estate advertisements, understand scientific texts, compare people and objects, make sentences giving reasons, talk about business life, and give a movie presentation.
courses.description: It includes teaching intermediate-level German grammar and vocabulary (Wortschatz) and preparation for the A2 level of the Goethe certificate exams, organized within the framework of the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages (CEFR).
learning_outcomes: Can write a short and simple note or message.
Can describe his/her activities and personal experiences.
He can talk about his future projects.
Can perform simple transactions in stores, post office or banks.
Can understand a personal letter that describes things from daily life and asks questions.
Can understand small advertisements in the newspaper. (house advertisements etc.)
Can write a composition of 120-150 words.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: A2.1 kurunda öğrenilen bilgilerin hatırlatılması ve tekrar çalışmaları
Hafta 2: Satın alacağı veya kiralayacağı evi betimleyebilmek, emlak ilanlarını okuyup anlayabilmek; sebep bildiren cümlelerin kullanulması (weil); kıyaslama cümleleri (wie-als)
Hafta 3: Sebep bildiren cümlelerin kullanulması (weil); kıyaslama cümleleri (wie-als)
Hafta 4: Kültür kavramı ile ilgili kelimelern öğrenilmesi; konuyla ilgili okuma parçalarının işlenmesi
Hafta 5: Dativ alan iyelik zamirleri (Possessivpronomen im Dativ); açıklama cümleleri (dass)
Hafta 6: İş hayatı ile ilgili kelimelerin öğrenilmesi; Genitiv konusunun öğrenilmesi; Alman gelenekleri ve bayram ritüellerinin öğrenilmesi; konuyla ilgili metinlerin okunması
Hafta 7: ARA SINAV
Hafta 8: Dönüşlü fiiller (Reflexive Verben)
Hafta 9: Beden dili ve duyguların ifadesi ile ilgili kelime çalışmaları; ödev sunumu
Hafta 10: Dolaylı soru ve aktarım cümleleri (Indirekte Rede –und Fragesaetze)
Hafta 11: Beden dili ve duyguların ifadesi ile ilgili 150 kelimelik bir kompozisyon yazma
Hafta 12: İcatlar hakkında konuşulması; bilimsel metin okuma çalışmaları
Hafta 13: Dativ alan şahıs zamirleri (Personalpronomen im Dativ)
Hafta 14: FİNAL SINAVI
resources: “MENSCHEN A.2.2” HUEBER VERLAG BERLİN (2012)
Billinca A., ReIMANN M.(2012)“ ÜBUNGSGRAMMATIK FÜR DIE GRUNDSTUFE“ HUEBER VERLAG
C., ROHRMANN L.(2006)” GRAMMATIK INTENSIVTRAINER A-2” LANGENSCHEIDT VERLAG
HÖRTEXTE UND EINFACHE LESESTÜCKE

courses.name: German V
courses.purpose: To be able to describe a past event in detail. To be able to introduce a person and their behavior, adding one’s own personal feelings. To be able to defend oneself in 10 sentences about whether an event is right or wrong. To be able to describe incidents happening around, such as accidents, murders, kidnappings, and the damage caused by natural disasters, and to convey this information to others. To be able to compare events and people.
courses.description: It includes teaching intermediate-advanced German grammar and vocabulary (Wortschatz) and preparation for the Goethe certificate exams at the B1 level, organized within the framework of the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages.
learning_outcomes: Can explain the subject of the book he read and express his reactions.
Can write a simple text explaining his interests and express his personal views.
Can understand what is said to him in everyday conversation when spoken clearly, but may ask for some phrases to be repeated.
He can complain.
Can write faxes, e-mails or short notes to his/her close circle.
Can write a composition of 150-180 words.

Hafta 2: -Zaman kavramı hakkında öğrencilerle diyalog kurma, konuyla ilgili kelimelerin öğrenilmesi -Edebi metinlere giriş -Geçmiş zamanın tekrarlanması

Hafta 4: -Konjunktiv 2 -Emir cümlelerinin tekrarlanması -Sebep bildiren cümlelerin tekrarlanması
Hafta 5: -Erkekler, kadınlar ve çiftler hakkında öğrencilerle diyalog kurma - Mastar cümlelerinin kullanımı (Infinitiv mit zu) -Olumsuzluk bildiren sıfatların kullanımı (Adjektive mit –los und –un)
Hafta 6: “BİLD” isimli dergiden bir makalenin seçilmesi ve anlatılması
Hafta 7: ARA SINAV

Hafta 9: Goethe B1 Sınavı Hazırlık çalışmaları 1.Bölüm -Dinleme-anlama -Okuma-anlama -Yazma

Hafta 11: -Konuyla ilgili kitaptaki metinlerin işlenmesi, soruların cevaplanması
Hafta 12: Ödev sunumu
Hafta 13: “DW” isimli dergiden bir makalenin seçilmesi ve anlatılması

courses.name: German VI
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop intermediate-advanced German language skills and prepare students for the Goethe certificate exams at the B1 level. It also focuses on enabling students to use a range of grammatical structures and communicative functions in written and spoken German.
courses.description: It includes teaching intermediate-advanced German grammar and vocabulary (Wortschatz) and preparation for the Goethe certificate exams at the B1 level, organized within the framework of the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages.
learning_outcomes: Can talk in detail about experiences they have had and describe their feelings and reactions.
Can describe his dreams, hopes and goals.
Can explain the plot of a movie and express reactions.
Can express himself clearly in predictable, familiar situations in daily life.
Can write formal texts that are not too difficult.
Can write a composition of 180-220 words.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: B1.1 kurunda öğrenilen bilgilerin hatırlatılması ve tekrar çalışmaları
Hafta 2: Öğrencilerle çevre ve çevre sorunları hakkında tartışılması, konuyla ilgili kelimelerin öğrenilmesi; konuya ilgili kompozisyon yazılması

Hafta 6: Goethe B1 Sınavı Hazırlık çalışmaları 2. Bölüm - Konuşma

Hafta 8: Öğrencilerle geçmiş kuşakların karşılaştırılması; edebi metinlerin incelenmesi; Passiv konusunun tekrarlanması
Hafta 9: Öğrencilerle göç ve göç sorunları hakkında tartışılması, konuyla ilgili kelimelerin öğrenilmesi; konuyla ilgili çeşitli makalelerin okunması
Hafta 10: Göç konusuyla ilgili bir filmin izlenmesi ve yorumlanması
Hafta 11: Öğrencilerle Avrupa, Avrupa Birliği ve politika konuları hakkında tartışılması, konuyla ilgili kelimelerin öğrenilmesi; konuyla ilgili çeşitli makalelerin okunması

Hafta 13: “BİLD” dergisinden seçilen bir makalenin incelenmesi ve tartışılması

courses.name: German VII
courses.purpose: To be able to read newspapers and articles about scientific subjects, understand and make comments, listen to and understand long interviews, write on forums and blogs, write private mails, and use German in working life situations such as telephone conversations, job fairs, and consultation talks. It also aims to develop discussion group techniques.
courses.description: To communicate comfortably and fluently with native German speakers, understand complex texts and discussions, express opinions on abstract topics, and present oneself in a clear and detailed way.
learning_outcomes: Be able to understand subject-specific and complicated articles
Be able to understand discussions about his specific field
Be able to talk with native speakers fluently and with no interrupts
Be able to talk with native speakers fluently and with no interrupts
Be able to explain the advantages and disadvantages of subjects
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Derse giriş – önceki kursun önemli konularının tekrarı

courses.name: Arabic I
courses.purpose: To develop basic listening, speaking, reading, and writing abilities in Arabic Language.
courses.description: A simplified introduction to the Arabic language, studying the letters and how to pronounce each letter with different vowels and positions, knowledge of some commonly used colors, days of the week, and months of the year. Study of some simple numbers and the method of counting, and an introduction to daily dialogues along with speaking, listening, writing, and reading practices. Knowledge of the daily greetings used and family affairs. A simplified study of pronouns in the Arabic language and some verbs and their types.
learning_outcomes: Use Arabic characteristics: single / plural / male / female, and classroom expressions.
Read and write the letters of the alphabet.
Read and write the days of the week, the months of the year, the colors and simple numbers.
Self introduction.
Learn greetings and use the most common everyday expressions on the street, at school and at home.
Use pronouns and different types of verbs used in the Arabic language.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: General information about the Arabic language -Classroom expressions: Very good/you read/read after me/open your book -Arabic characteristics: single / plural / male / female
Hafta 2: General study of the Arabic alphabet as all, (Phonetics) -The shape and the method of writing of each letter. -The sound of each letter as general
Hafta 3: The sound and pronunciation of each letter in details. -The first seven letters of the Arabic alphabet, their pronunciation and sounds, writing and their reading. - Classroom expressions.
Hafta 4: The second seven letters of the Arabic alphabet. -Their pronunciation and sounds, writing and their reading. - Conversation and pronunciation drills.

Hafta 5: Writing exercise -The third seven letters of the Arabic alphabet -Their pronunciation and sounds, writing and their reading. - Knowing how to write and read these letters in their different positions in the word. -Grammar corner: What does the sentence consist of?

Hafta 6: The last seven letters of the Arabic alphabet -Their pronunciation and sounds, writing and their reading. - Knowing how to write and read these letters in their different positions in the word. -Grammar corner: What types of the sentence?
Hafta 7: -Review phonetics -Review sentences
Hafta 8: The different vowels of the Arabic letters. -The meaning of tanween in Arabic. -Pronunciation and conversation drills
Hafta 9: The days of the week in Arabic. -Reading comprehension.
Hafta 10: The months of the year. -Knowledge of colors. -Numbers: 1-10
Hafta 11: How to write and pronounce the numbers (1-10). -Conversation and pronunciation drills -Reading comprehension -Greetings: Hello, good morning, Good evening, goodbye, how are you?

Hafta 12: The most daily expressions commonly used on the street: at school, and at home -How are you?. Thank you, Please, Can you help me?, What time is it?, I'm sorry, How much does it cost?, I do not understand, No problem, Watch out. -Grammar corner: the pronouns used in the Arabic language.

Hafta 13: The most daily expressions commonly used at the school and home - Why are you late today?. Stop talking in the class, It’s a time for break, take care of our sister, eat our lunch please, tidy up our room please, Do not watch television for long time please. -Grammar corner: the different types of verbs in Arabic.
Hafta 14: Conversation and pronunciation drills. -Reading comprehension. -Listening.
resources: Habash, N.Y., 2022. Introduction to Arabic natural language processing. Springer Nature.

Ghani, S.A., Hashim, U.H.H., Shaadon, Z. and Mustapa, N.S., 2012. The purpose of reading arabic text and the problems faced: a preliminary survey at the faculty of Islamic studies. Procedia-Social and Behavioral Sciences, 59, pp.18-25.

courses.name: Innov. Leadership in Comp.Eng.
courses.purpose: The aim of the course is to increase awareness in creativity and charisma for the future potential leaders. By means of gaining these abilities, students will have wider horizons that will enable them to analyze the leadership situations at companies and therefore they will be able to challenge the difficulties of business life.
courses.description: The course focuses on creativity, innovation, charisma, and leadership. It aims to help students think innovatively, understand charismatic leadership, improve self-awareness, and develop presentation and writing skills through exercises and short story analysis.
learning_outcomes: The student will increase the self-awareness for creativity and will be able to contribute more to his/her daily life with the exercises to be done during the classes as well as outside.
The student’s awareness for charisma will increase and will be able to synthetize it with leadership roles in the future in business life.
The student will learn the crucial elements of leadership and will realize the resources to use in business life.
The student will learn different presentation skills which will contribute to any presentations he or she may be doing in the future.

courses.name: Social Security Law
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach the social security rights and obligations of dependent workers, public servants, and self-employed persons, as well as the legal rights provided against social risks such as illness, maternity, disability, unemployment, old age, and death. It also introduces the conditions required to benefit from these rights.
courses.description: The course covers the protection of individuals against occupational accidents and diseases, illness, maternity, disability, old age, death, unemployment, and other social risks. It examines the social insurance mechanisms established by the state under social security law as a constitutional duty.
learning_outcomes: Acquire basic knowledge in social security law and learn methods of accessing legal sources, obtaining information, and interpreting them in order to understand the purpose of legal rules and develop a just perspective.
Examine enterprises’ issues related to labour relations and social security using scientific methods, interpret data, and evaluate results.
Relate the theoretical and practical foundations gained in social security law to knowledge acquired in other fields.

Hafta 2: Insurance holders (1): Employees
Hafta 3: Insurance holders (2): Public servants and self-employed ones
Hafta 4: Financing of social security
Hafta 5: Insurance of occupational accidents and diseases

Hafta 7: Insurance of disability
Hafta 8: Old-age insurance (1)

courses.name: Syndicate and Collective Bargaining Law
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach students the principles and rules regarding the establishment and functioning of unions, the guarantees of the union right, the scope of collective bargaining agreements in terms of place, persons and time, the authority and process of collective bargaining, and strike and lockout.
courses.description: The birth of labor law was concurrent with workers gaining bargaining power against employers under the umbrella of the union organization. Otherwise, the understanding of protecting workers through laws that can only be changed in favor of the workers emerged after the Second World War. With the union organization, the workers determined their working conditions imperative in the collective bargaining agreements that emerged as a result of a collective bargain with the employer, and they were able to put pressure on the employer by strike during the negotiation phase.
learning_outcomes: Consciousness about the importance of law in a society and in this respect, especially, to develop their understanding of social responsibility.
Knowledge on concepts and institutions regarding different law diciplines.
Capability to interpret and assess legislation, case-law, doctrine and to solve legal problems for implementation of the theoretical knowledge gained.
Consciousness about the importance of the team work.
Perspective for the production of projects.
Perspective about life-long learning and renewing oneself.

courses.name: Occupational Safety and Health Law
courses.purpose: In this course, the role of state in occupational health and safety, especially its duties of making legislation and surveillance, rights and duties of employers and employees and compensation of work accidents' results will be considered.
courses.description: Protection of human life is the basic duty of the state. Since work exposes individuals to occupational risks, special protection of working people's health, is exceptionally important in labour law. Although protection of working people's health is a matter of medicine and engineering, as a duty of the employer, occupational health and safety is also a matter of law.
learning_outcomes: Consciousness about the importance of law in a society and in this respect, especially, to develop their understanding of social responsibility.
Knowledge on concepts and institutions regarding different law diciplines.
Capability to interpret and assess legislation, case-law, doctrine and to solve legal problems for implementation of the theoretical knowledge gained.
Perspective about life-long learning and renewing oneself.

courses.name: Protection of Technological Innovations
courses.purpose: To introduce intellectual property rights, which are the tools of the protection of technological innovations, in general. To describe the remedies against infringement of intellectual property rights. To teach major intellectual property rights regimes and their interconnection.

learning_outcomes: Understand the appropriate legal tools to be used for the protection of the ideas
Ability to understand and supervise the measures to be taken for the acquisition of the intellectual property right
Ability to prima facie decide whether the conducts of third parties constitute the infringement of the intellectual property right
Ability to decide and administer the proper tools for commercializing the ideas

courses.name: R&D Law
courses.purpose: The main aim of our course is to provide a basic legal knowledge that is needed by our engineers who are on the production side of R&D and technology, and our entrepreneurs with creative ideas. In addition to this, it is to deal with the legal aspects of the relations between all sides of this multi-party R&D and technology production actions.
courses.description: In the digital age where competition is prominent, the most important competitive advantage in commercial life is of course possible with the production of technological products with high added value. The main way to achieve this is through R&D and being a company focused on technology production. Being able to do R&D is a multilateral relationship that directly concerns those who do it, those who have it done, and those who use the outputs of R&D. In this R&D Law course, we will discuss these relations, the rights and obligations of the parties.
learning_outcomes: Consciousness about the importance of law in a society and in this respect, especially, to develop their understanding of social responsibility.
Knowledge on concepts and institutions regarding different law diciplines
Reflections of technology, R&D and the outcomes of R&D on commercial and social life, the establishment of these relations and maintaining these relations in a healthy way.
Consciousness about the importance of the team work
Perspective for the production of projects
Perspective about life-long learning and renewing oneself

courses.name: Commercial Law
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with basic knowledge about the main actors of commercial life, such as merchants, commercial enterprises, companies, unfair competition, and intellectual property rights. It also aims to help students understand the rights and obligations among these parties so that they can make more informed decisions in their professional lives and protect themselves legally.
courses.description: The main players of commercial life are companies or merchants and people who interact with them. The main purpose of commercial law is to determine the relations between these persons and the rights and obligations of the parties. In this context, it is now a must for all persons, whether entrepreneurs, employees, investors or consumers, to be informed about the term merchant, provisions regarding becoming a merchant, and companies and other key players in commercial life. These relationships and their reflections in life constitute the subject of our lesson.
learning_outcomes: Consciousness about the importance of law in a society and in this respect, especially, to develop their understanding of social responsibility.
Knowledge on concepts and institutions regarding different law disciplines.
Capability to interpret and assess legislation, case-law, doctrine and to solve legal problems for implementation of the theoretical knowledge gained.
Informed decision making skills about legal situations or issues they will face while performing their profession.

courses.name: Spanish I
courses.purpose: Students will learn to build simple sentences, introduce themselves, talk about their likes and preferences, their hobbies and daily life. They will also learn the simple present form, describe people, and express likes, dislikes, opinions, agreement or disagreement in a simple way.
courses.description: Basic Spanish vocabulary and grammar and the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages A1 level preparatory work.
learning_outcomes: Will be able to present themselves and to greet and say goodbye in a simple way
Will be able to ask people personal information about themselves
Will be able to describe and talk about themselves and other people using simple sentences
Will be able to describe what there is and there is not in several places such as their cities and neighbourhoods
Talk about what they like to do in their free time
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: - First salutations: greetings and farewell: ¡Hola, buenos días!, !Adiós, hasta mañana! - How to introduce yourself and how to ask another person about themselves : (Subjects I and you): name, surname, origin,… Construction of small dialogues in pairs. Practice with a video on the Internet. - Some Interrogative pronouns I: “¿Dónde vices?, ¿De dónde eres?,... - Demonstrative pronouns: “este”, “esta”,... Examples with famous Spanish speakers

Hafta 2: - Alphabet. Practice with a video on the Internet. - Control of communication: How do you say "..." in Spanish?, What does it mean "..."?, How do you pronounce "..."?, How do you spell "..."?, Can you repeat, please?, I do not understand... - Pronounciation: Special reference to letters with various sounds such as “c”, “g”,…

Hafta 3: - Numbers from 0 to 100: “bingo game”. Phone numbers and e-mail address. - Countries, nationalities and cities. Verbs “Ser” and “Vivir”. Practice with a Spanish song. - Names of professions. Verbs “trabajar” and “estudiar”.

Hafta 4: - Some Interrogative pronouns II: “¿A qué te dedicas?”,“¿Qué haces?”,... Introduction to the rules of concordance according to gender and numbers: masculine, feminine and plural. General rules and exceptions. Brief introduction to the definite articles: “el”, “la”, “los”, “las”.

Hafta 5: - Hobbies and free time activities. Introcution to spanish conjugation: “ –ar, -er, -ir” as infinitive terminations. - Subject pronouns. Polite forms: “Usted” and “Ustedes”. - Introduction to the regular simple present tense (Presente de indicativo regular). Practice with songs and exercices.

Hafta 6: - Justified interests and desires. Why do you learn Spanish?: Uso de “por qué”, “porque”, “para” and “por”. - Like, dislike and interests I: Verbs “querer”, “amar”, “odiar” y “preferir”. Ser aficionado a + adjetivo. Review of the concordance rules (number and gender): “peferido/a” and “favorito/a”.
Hafta 7: MIDTERM

Hafta 8: - Like, dislike and interests II: Pronominal verbs “gustar”, “encantar”, “interesar” + article/infinitive. Practice with a Spanish song. - Using of quantifiers: “mucho”, “nada”, “poco” y “bastante”.
Hafta 9: Coincidence or difference in hobbies and interests. Use of “también” and “tampoco”. -Practice: Holiday Survey.

Hafta 10: - Differences between “ser” and “estar” I: Physical states and temporal situations. - Differences between “ser” and “estar” II: How to indicate the position of objects in the space. General placement and space prepositions: “en”, “encima de”, “debajo de”... - Vocabulary: introduction to the introduction to the furniture of the house

Hafta 11: - Differences between “ser” and “estar” III: “Ser”, “tener” y “llevar” to describe people. Review of the concordance rules (number and gender). - Adjectives for physical description and character. - Vocabulary: clothes and colors.
Hafta 12: - Family vocabulary and possesive pronouns: “mi”, “tu”, “su”,... Practice with the Spanish Royal Family. - Use of the verb “parecerse”. Difference between “me parece”, “parece”, “parecen”.

Hafta 13: - Using of the impersonal verb “hay” for existence. Contrast with the verb “tener”. - Brief reference to the neighborhood vocabulary. - Use of the quantifiers: “mucho/a”, “poco/a”, “bastante/s”,... - Distinction in the use of “ser”, “estar”, “tener” and “hay”. Exemplification of the uses of these verbs in relation to the description of a country, a city and a village. - Using rules of definite and undefinite articles. - Brief reference to the weather vocabulary.
Hafta 14: FINAL EXAM

Explain experiences and actions in the past connected with the present.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Remembering the highlights of the first level I: a) Revising pronominal verbs: “gustar”, “encantar”, “interesar” + article/infinitive. b) Differences between “ser” and “estar” I. Description, location and physical states. c) Remembering the highlights of the first level II: Regular Simple Present.

Hafta 2: Irregular simple present tense I (Presente de indicativo irregular): Introduction to the irregular simple present tense. Study of the different groups of irregular verbs according to their vowel change. Working with sentences: Construction of sentences. Accentuation of the particularity of the Spanish in the use of direct object. “A preposition” + personal direct object. Irregular simple present tense II (Presente de indicativo irregular). Working the irregular verbs through songs and exercices.

Hafta 3: Irregular simple present tense I (Presente de indicativo irregular): Introduction to the irregular simple present tense. Study of the different groups of irregular verbs according to their vowel change. Working with sentences: Construction of sentences. Accentuation of the particularity of the Spanish in the use of direct object. “A preposition” + personal direct object. Irregular simple present tense II (Presente de indicativo irregular). Working the irregular verbs through songs and exercices.
Hafta 4: Time and schedules. “¿Qué hora es?” and “¿A qué hora...?”, days, weeks, months. Habits and routines. Introduction to reflexive verbs. Practice with videos and songs.
Hafta 5: Sequencing actions: “primero”, “luego”, “antes de + infinitivo”, “después de + infinitivo”, “finalmente”,... Short film.
Hafta 6: Expressions of frequency: “Siempre”, “Nunca”, “A veces”, “Normalmente”,... Writing a letter in Spanish: explanation of the daily routines.
Hafta 7: MIDTERM

Hafta 8: Differences between “ser” and “estar” II. Description, location and physical states. Another way to express physical states: “tener + nombre”. Explain the symptoms of a disease to a doctor. Verb “doler” and “tener dolor de”.

Hafta 9: The present continuous in Spanish. “Estar + gerundio”. Functions, uses and differences with the presente simple tense. Present continuous for reflexive verbs. Practice with songs and exercices. Relative Clause. Identification of people through adjectives, names or verbs.

Hafta 10: Past tense I: Pretérito imperfecto simple. Conjugation. Practice with songs and exercices. Uses: Habits and routines in the past. Verb “Soler”. Description in the past. Likes, dislikes and hobbies in the past. Contrast between present and past (before and after): “Antes/después de casarme, antes/después de tener un hijo,...”.
Hafta 11: Time expressions: “De pequeño”, “A los 10 años”, “Cuando era más joven”, “Cuando iba a la universidad”,... The students will write a composition about their own childhood.

Hafta 12: Past tense II (Introduction): Pretérito perfecto Compuesto. “Haber + participio pasado”. Uses: Past actions without saying when they occurred. Things that have just happened. Personal experiences.

Hafta 13: Time expressions: “Hoy”, “Esta tarde/mañana/noche/semana”, “Este mes/año…”, “Hace un momento”,… Frequency: “Muchas veces”, “Alguna vez”, “Nunca”, “Todavía”, “Ya”,... Practice with videos on the internet.
Hafta 14: FINAL EXAM
