Neus Sans Baulenas, Ernesto Martín Peris (2003). Gente 1 Nueva Edición. Ed. Difusión. S.L.

courses.name: Spanish III
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students explain past events in detail, use basic subordinate clauses and connectors, express themselves in simple conversations, understand dialogue topics, describe and understand biographies, make oral presentations, and summarize a book.
courses.description: Pre-Intermediate level Spanish grammar and vocabulary, and preparations for A2 level of DELE exams in line with Common European Framework of Reference.
learning_outcomes: Speak about facts in the past combining the 3 more important past tenses in Spanish language. They will be able to use simple conjunctions like: “because”, “although”, “even”, “yet”, “that” and build simple subordinate sentences.
Students will talk about the qualities of their profession.
Talk about their homes now and before and compare life in the town and in the city.
Use personal pronouns of “complemento directo” and “indirecto”.
Dialogue in a restaurant and supermarket.
Students will write a postcard explaining what they have done and their plans.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: - Proffesions. - Definitions of several professions. Review present simple. - Work schedules and frequent actions of different professions: “A las”,” De... a”, “Desde las... hasta las...”, “Entre las... y las...”) - Necessary qualities and requirements to perform a profession: “Hay que”, “Tener que”, “Es bueno/importante/necesario + infinitivo”.

Hafta 2: - Habilities and skills. Verbs “poder” and “saber”. - The students will explain the necessary qualities to satisfactorily perform their favourite professions. -Listening about the topic. -Jobs in the Spanish culture.

Hafta 3: - Restaurants and supermarkets. Meals, food and eating habits. - Order in a restaurant or supermarket: “Me pone…”, “Quería…”. - Vocabulary related to weights and measures: un kilo, cien gramos, un trozo, una barra, un carton, una lata,… - Quantifiers: “(Un) poco (de”), “bastante”, “mucho”, “nada (de)”. - Listening in a Spanish bar. - Examples and representation of short dialogues in restaurants and supermarkets.

Hafta 4: - Vocabulary connected with the house. Description of the house, the rooms and furnishings. The stundents will compare their houses with other people’s houses or with the house of their childhood.
Hafta 5: - Comparative. “mas ... que”, “menos ... que”, “tan....como”... - Verbs: “quedar” and “quedarse”. - Present Continuous Review.

Hafta 6: - Review past tense II: Pretérito perfecto Compuesto. “Haber + participio pasado”. Uses: - Past actions without saying when they occurred. - Things that have just happened. - Personal experiences. Time expressions: “Hoy”, “Esta tarde/mañana/noche/semana”, “Este mes/año…”, “Hace un momento”,… Frequency: “Muchas veces”, “Alguna vez”, “Nunca”, “Todavía”, “Ya”,... - Demostrativos: este”, “ese”, “aquel”,… - The student will talk about work and positive free time experiences. “Pasarlo bien/mal”, “divertirse”, “disfrutar”. Suprise and exclamations “¡Qué....!”. - Propose and reject plans: “¿Te/os/le/les apetece…?”, “Vale”, “Buena idea” , “Es que… Pues no sé…”. - Future verbal periphrase: “ir a + infinitivo”. Family or personal, work and holidays plans. Time expressions: “Luego”, “Más tarde”, “Esta mañana”, “Esta noche”, “Dentro de un mes”, “Pasado mañana”, “La semana/el mes/el año que viene”,... - The students will write a postcard, an email or a letter explainin a trip.
Hafta 7: MIDTERM
Hafta 8: - Past tense II: Pretérito indefinido. Conjugation. Presentation and practice of the most common irregular. Practice with songs and exercices.
Hafta 9: - Uses: - Daily activities in the past. - Biographies. - Historical events.

Hafta 10: - Time expressions and dates: “Ayer”, “Hace dos días”, “El otro día”,... - Combination of and “pretérito imperfecto” and “pretérito indefinido. Talk of the cause and consequence of actions. - Connectors: “como”, “porque”, “por eso”, “entonces”, “pero”,... - The students will write a biography or/and a composition explaining what they do on the last weekend.

Hafta 11: - Contrast of the three past tenses. Review of the construction and basic functions of each time. Highlight the function of “indefinido” for the action and “imperfecto” for the description. -Explanation of a little story using both “indefinido” and “imperfecto” properly.
Hafta 12: - Introduction to direct object pronouns: “lo”, “la”, “los”, “las” and reference to the indirect object pronoun “le”, “les”. Explanation, exercices and practice.
Hafta 13: The transformation of the pronoun "le" to "se".
Hafta 14: FINAL EXAM
resources: Jaime Corpas, Eva García, Agustín Garmendia (2020). Aula Internacional Plus 2. Curso de Español. Difusión. S.L. Barcelona.
Jaime Corpas, Eva García, Agustín Garmendia (2020). Aula Internacional Plus 1. Curso de Español. Difusión. S.L. Barcelona.
Jaime Corpas, Eva García, Agustín Garmendia (2020). Aula Internacional Plus 2. Curso de Español. Difusión. S.L. Barcelona.

courses.name: Spanish IV
courses.purpose: The students will be able to: 1. Express difficulties and give advices to overcome them. 2. Express what they want and what they need; also their opinion and the condition of some facts. 3. Compare things. 4. Give instructions. 5. Talk about what was happening in a period of time in a complex way. 6. Talk about an unknown future. 7. Express their positive or negative habits.
courses.description: It includes teaching intermediate Spanish grammar and vocabulary and the second level of preparatory work for the DELE exams organized within the framework of the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages (CEFR) at the A2 level.
learning_outcomes: Talk about their language learning process.
Give advices or orders.
Do shopping and ask more complex questions about products.
Use pronouns correctly.
Explain stories, films, legends or jokes in the past in a more accurate way.
Speak about future predictions, assumptions and conditions.
Use "basic periphrasis" of Spanish language to explain habits.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: - Learning a foreign language. A) Express difficulties: “Me cuesta/n” “Me siento…”, “ Para mí, es difícil…”. B) Recommendations: “Tienes que + inf.”, “Lo mejor es + inf.”, “Va muy bien + inf.”, “Van muy bien + nombres en plural”. -How to make questions. Exercices. The student can write an interview for a famous person.

Hafta 2: - Affirmative ad negative imperative. Conjugation of regular and irregular verbs. Practice with songs and videos on the internet. Uses: - Recommendations and advices: “For a clean city”. - Instructions and orders: Reading a map, ask and find directions. Explaining the way.
Hafta 3: - Placement of direct, indirect and impersonal pronouns with the imperative. “Le/se”, “lo”, “la”, “las”, “los”, “les/se”.
Hafta 4: - Impersonal forms: “Se + 3ª persona”. Recipes: “cortar”, “pelar”, “freir”,... - The student will write a recipe with both “se impersonal” and “imperative”.

Hafta 5: - Contrast of the three past tenses II: Review of the construction and functions of the three past tenses learned in the previous courses. Theory and exercises. - Explain stories in present and past tense. Tell legends and jokes. Prepare the synopsis of a film. The students will write about an special event in their lives and about an important event in the history of Turkey.

Hafta 6: Structured the speech with connectors: - Sequence: “De repente”, “De pronto”, “Entonces”, “En aquel momento”, “Al final” - Cause: “Como”, “Porque”, “Es que” - Contrast: “Pero”, “Sin embargo”, “Aunque”…
Hafta 7: MIDTERM

Hafta 8: Shopping. Dialogues in stores in order to buy and sell clothes. - Vocabulary and verbs related to shopping: “pagar”, “precios”, “costar”, “más grande”, “más pequeño”, “hay”, “no hay”, “tienen”, “no tienen”... - Systems of ellipsis and relative clauses: “artículo + adjetivo”, “artículo + de +…”. “La amarrilla”,“La de cuadros” - Examples and representation of short dialogues about shopping. - Review of the direct and indirect object pronouns.
Hafta 9: - Routines in the present: “volver a”, “dejar de”, “ponerse a”, “estar a punto de”, “seguir + gerundio”, “seguir sin + infinitivo”.
Hafta 10: - Explain past experiences: “ Estaba + gerundio”, “Estuve + gerundio” - Duration of an action and time elapsed from the beginning: “hace”, “desde hace”, “desde que”…

Hafta 11: - Future tense: “El Futuro Imperfecto”. Conjugation. Regular /irregular verbs. Uses: -Assumptions: “Seguramente”, “Probablemente”, “Posiblemente”, “Seguro que”, “Supongo que”… -Predictions: “Mañana subirán las temperaturas.” -Conditions with a result in the future: “Si estudias, aprobarás.”
Hafta 12: Time expressions: mañana, pasado mañana, el sábado, dentro de 2 meses, el lunes que viene”, “el lunes próximo”… * “Ir + a” for plans.

Hafta 13: - Other ways of expressing conditions: “Si + presente, + presente”: Si llueve mañana, me quedo en casa. “Depende de + sustantivo”: Depende de mi trabajo. “Depende de si + presente de indicativo”: Depende de si termino el informe. -Practice with songs and videos on the internet.
Hafta 14: FINAL EXAM

courses.name: Spanish V
courses.purpose: The course aims to help students talk about past events, express actions that started in the past and continue in the present, and use advanced Spanish structures such as past tenses, subjunctive, imperative, and expressions of obligation, permission, wishes, feelings, and needs.
courses.description: It includes teaching intermediate Spanish grammar and vocabulary and preparing students for the B1 level of the DELE exams, which are organized within the framework of the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages (CEFR).
learning_outcomes: Express a previous past.
Negative orders.
Express permission and obligation.
Express wishes, complaints and needs.
Express feelings.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Review of the main tenses learnt in the previous courses I: Presente simple regular e irregular (oral) Presente continuo (oral) Pretérito imperfecto, Pretérito indefinido y Pretérito perfecto (oral) - Review of the simultaneous use of the three past tenses. Exercices. - Review of the main tenses learnt in the previous courses II: Futuro imperfecto Pasado continuo - Review of personal pronouns: Pronombres de objeto directo Pronombres de objeto directo Pronombre reflexivos “Se impersonal”

Hafta 2: - Presentation of a new past tense “pretérito pluscuamperfecto”: Conjugation of verb “to have” and review of irregular past participles. Connectors: así que, de modo que, como, porque,... Ways of structuring information: resulta que, total que, al final que,... Interactional behavior: (¡Ajá! ¡Ya! ¡Claro! ¡Lógico! ¡Normal!, ¿De verdad? ¡No me digas! ¿Ah, sí? ¡No!), pena (¡Vaya! ¡Qué pena/rabia/horror/rollo/mal!, ¡Menos mal! ¡Qué bien/buena suerte!).

Hafta 3: -Communicative functions of language put into practice: Relate past events Talk about causes and consequences in the past Show interest when listenig to a story or anecdote Interaction in a conversation -Practice with songs and videos on the internet.
Hafta 4: - Presente de subjuntivo. Conjugation of regular and irregular verbs.

Hafta 5: Uses: - Wishes and complaints: “Exigimos que el presidente dimita” - Necessity: “Necesitamos que bajen los impuestos”. -“Cuando / antes de que + subjuntivo”: “Yo me iré cuando tú vengas”, “Debes de recapacitar antes de que sea demasiado tarde”…
Hafta 6: - Solutions and claims: “Debería”, “Se debería”, “Habría que”, “Tenemos que”.. -Practice with songs and videos on the internet.
Hafta 7: MIDTERM
Hafta 8: - Imperative. - Review of the affirmative imperative. - Negative imperative. Coinciding with “presente de subjuntivo”.

Hafta 9: Uses: - Advice: “no bebas alcohol si vas a conducir.” - Order: “No conduzcas por la izquierda.” - Instruction: “No sigas por esa calle.” - Permission: “No pienses más en él.” Describe an advertisement.
Hafta 10: -Prohibition: “Se prohibe”, “No se admite”, “No se puede”… -Obligatoriness: “Es obligatorio”…
Hafta 11: -Habits: “Lo normal”, “Lo habitual”, “Lo más frecuente”, “Lo más raro”… -Impersonality: “se + verbo en tercera persona”.

Hafta 12: - Verbs to express interests and feelings (Infinitive or subjunctive). -Typical Expressions: Me alegra mucho que mis estudiantes siempre hagan los deberes. Estoy muy contenta de que hayas venido a visitarme. Estoy harta de que me interrumpas cuando hablo. Me molesta que los estudiantes lleguen tarde a clase. Me interesa que mis estudiantes se sientan motivados en clase porque así aprenden más. Me gusta que participéis en clase de forma igualitaria. Me apasiona que las personas tengan intereses relacionados con el arte. No me importa que la gente hable mal de mí. Me fascina que todos los elementos del universo estén en perfecta armonía o perfecto caos. Me entusiasma que mis estudiantes se pregunten por las razones de las cosas. Me horroriza que/Me da miedo que las personas se dejen llevar por los principios capitalistas. Me irrita que los estudiantes no se escuchen los unos a los otros en clase. Me sienta bien que mis seres queridos estén felices. Me pone nerviosa que alguien intente controlar mi vida. Me hace ilusión que me regalen flores. Me hace gracia que mis estudiantes hablen español mejor que yo. - Substantive subordinate clauses - Subjunctive mode selection - Interactional behavior: Intensification of agreement (¡Claro!) or disagreement with the interlocutor (¡Qué va !), Diminish the threatening act (Lo que pasa es que,..), Eloquent values of the interrogatives statements (Pues..).
Hafta 13: Communicative functions of language put into practice: Show resounding and softened agreement Submit a counter-argument Express likes and interests Express wishes and feelings
Hafta 14: FINAL EXAM
resources: Jaime Corpas, Eva García, Agustín Garmendia (2020). Aula Internacional Plus 3. Curso de Español. Difusión. S.L. Barcelona.
Alonso, R. Catañeda, A. Martínez, P. Miquel, L. Ortega, J. y Ruiz, J.P. (2005). Gramática Básica del Estudiante de Español. Ed. Difusión. S.L. Barcelona.
Palencia del Burgo, R. y Aragonés Fernández, L. (2014) Gramática de Uso del Español. B1-B2: Teoría y práctica con solucionario. Ed. Difusión. S.L. Barcelona.

courses.name: Spanish VI
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop intermediate Spanish language skills and prepare students for the B1 level of the DELE exams within the CEFR framework.
courses.description: It includes teaching intermediate Spanish grammar and vocabulary and preparing students for the B1 level of the DELE exams, which are organized within the framework of the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages (CEFR).
learning_outcomes: To report other people’s messages and hold telephone conversations.
To make descriptions using relative clauses and other complex expressions in Spanish.
To express hypotheses.
To narrate mysterious events; to give opinions and make assessments about them.
To use the present indicative, the present subjunctive, and the conditional according to their standard usage to express wishes, hypotheses, certainty and doubt, or to make assertions.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Reported Speech. Indirect style and its changes. The verbs go, come, bring, bring. Verbs that introduce referred style: say, ask, ask, etc., Special reference to the use of the subjunctive. - Transmit message from others: -Transmitir una información: Me ha dicho que, me ha comentado que,… -Transmitir una pregunta: Me ha preguntado si/ qué/ cómo/ cuál/… -Transmitir una intención:

Hafta 2: Hold telephone conversations and leave messages. -Responder: “¿Dígame?” -Preguntar por alguien: “Hola, ¿está Javier?” -Identificar a la persona que llama: “¿De parte de quién? -Pasar una llamada: “Ahora se pone”

Hafta 3: Tomar un mensaje: -Transmit orders, advice and requests: “Le ha dicho que pase esta tarde por su casa”, “Le ha pedido que le ayude a hacer los ejercicios”, “Le ha recomendado que se compre un coche nuevo”…

Hafta 4: Relative Clauses. -Con indicativo o subjuntivo: “Quería ver esa cámara del escaparate que cuesta 200 euros” /”Quería ver una cámara que cueste 200 euros.” -Con preposición: “Este es el coche con el que fuimos a Murcia”, “Los hoteles en los que nos alojamos tenían vistas al mar.”,...

Hafta 5: Talk about the operation and characteristics of a thing. Sirve para, Se usa para, Funciona/ Va con, Consume, Ocupa, Cabe en, Dura, No se arruga, No se estropea, No pasa de moda,.., -Value a thing. Lo encuentro/veo, No me desagrada, No me convence, ¡Qué horror!,…
Hafta 6: Use adjectives in all their grades (including the superlative). Buenísimo, carísimo, feísimo, riquísimo, superpráctico,...
Hafta 7: MIDTERM
Hafta 8: The simple future to express hypothesis. ¿Dónde estará Juan? Son las doce, estará trabajando en su oficina.

Hafta 9: Express certainty, security and uncertainty and doubt. Formulate hypotheses about the present and the recent past. -Con indicativo: Seguro que, Seguramente, Supongo que, A lomejor, Estoy seguro/a de que,... -Con subjuntivo: Lo más seguro es que, Es probable que, Es posible que, Puede que, ... -Con indicativo y subjuntivo: Tal vez, quizá, probablemente, posiblemente,...
Hafta 10: Narrate mysterious facts. Express belief, opinion, agreement, disagreement and valuation before events and testimonies.

Hafta 11: The simple conditional. -Expresar deseos: Me encantaría, me gustaría,… -Opinar sobre acciones y conductas: “Nunca viajaría a La Luna”, “¿Irías a cenar a casa de un desconocido?” -Evocar situaciones hipotéticas: “¿Qué harías para mejorar la situación medioambiental?” “Pondríauna tasa turista”,… -Aconsejar y sugerir: “Deberías reciclar el papel. Para ello, podrías comprarte una papelera especial.”
Hafta 12: Assess situations and facts.

Hafta 13: The present indicative: Es verdad/cierto/evidente que… -The present subjunctive: (No) es/ (No) me parece + lógico, necesario, suficiente, grave, normal, importante, estupendo, terribel,…+ que -Infinitive: (No) está bien/mal que, (No) me parece bien/mal que, Es logico/necesario/…que,…
Hafta 14: FINAL EXAM
resources: Jaime Corpas, Eva García, Agustín Garmendia (2020). Aula Internacional Plus 3. Curso de Español. Difusión. S.L. Barcelona.
Alonso, R. Catañeda, A. Martínez, P. Miquel, L. Ortega, J. y Ruiz, J.P. (2005). Gramática Básica del Estudiante de Español. Ed. Difusión. S.L. Barcelona.
Palencia del Burgo, R. y Aragonés Fernández, L. (2014) Gramática de Uso del Español. B1-B2: Teoría y práctica con solucionario. Ed. Difusión. S.L. Barcelona.

courses.name: Italian I
courses.purpose: The course aims to help students learn basic Italian communication skills for everyday situations. Students develop the ability to introduce themselves, ask and answer simple questions, and handle routine interactions such as shopping, invitations, and dining.
courses.description: Includes basic grammar and vocabulary of the Italian language and a starting preparation to the level A1 according to the Common European Framework of Reference (CEFR).
learning_outcomes: The students that pass this course will be able to describe and talk about themselves and other people using simple sentences, and will be able to produce a writing of at least 60 words.
Will be able to write an e-mail of invitation/presentation using short and simple sentences.
Will be able to read and understand small announcements, catalogues, orders and short sentences.
Will be able to present themselves and to greet and say goodbye in a simple way.
Will be able to ask people about themselves.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: course introduction alphabet Italian and Turkish cultural aspects Italy in Turkey

Hafta 2: Introduce yourself (your name, surname, nationality) - Greetings - Ask questions formally and informally - Subject pronouns - The verb “to be” - To be/to exist - Verbs for calling oneself - Subject pronouns - The verb “to be” - To be/to exist - Verbs for calling oneself

Hafta 3: Gender and number of nouns and adjectives - Noun-adjective agreement - Negative and interrogative sentences - School - Nations and nationalities - Numbers from zero to twenty - Gender and number of nouns and adjectives - Noun-adjective agreement - Negative and interrogative sentences

Hafta 4: Don't ask or say how you are (How are you? - How are you? - I'm fine/bad/so-so...) - Expressing your mood -Regular verbs in ARE - Verbs of possession, verbs of being - Idiomatic expressions related to being and possession
Hafta 5: Expressing a physical state - Expressing quantity Quantity adverbs: very, enough, little, not at all, too much - Indefinite and definite articles: un/il, un/lo, una/la, un/l', un'/
Hafta 6: Invite a friend - Ask questions and provide information about your life situation regular herbs
Hafta 7: - ERE - To go verb - Idiomatic expressions with Andar
Hafta 8: Express the frequency of an action - Express quality Frequency adverbs (I): never, sometimes, often, always, usually - Plural definitions - Prepositions of place (I): in, a, d

Hafta 9: Ask for and give information about your job and/or field of work -IRE-regular verbs Irregular verbs: to come, to do, to give. Idiomatic expressions with the verb “to do” - Article, noun, adjective agreement
Hafta 10: Ask and tell what time it is - Ask and tell the date - Names in -ista Prepositions of place (II): from - Prepositions of time (I) - Preposition: indication
Hafta 11: Order - Ask about the price - Request an invoice - Irregular verbs: to say, to drink, to go out
Hafta 12: Express likes and preferences - Ask for permission I like/I don't like. I liked/I didn't like -, for, on, between/among prepositions - Unchangeable nouns
Hafta 13: How to behave in a café or restaurant; food, drinks, and objects
Hafta 14: - Restaurant, trattoria, pizzeria - I want/I would like... - I can... At the bar.

courses.name: Italian II
courses.purpose: The course aims to develop students’ basic Italian communication skills by building vocabulary and grammar needed for everyday situations. It prepares students to express themselves about routines, preferences, directions, family, and past events, and to produce simple oral and written texts.
courses.description: Includes intermediate grammar and vocabulary of the Italian language and a starting preparation to the level A1 according to the Common European Framework of Reference (CEFR).
learning_outcomes: will understand a general conversation related to a familiar topic
will be able to talk about self, family, daily routines, and past events
will be able to talk about their country and its habits about different meanings
will be able to understand and produce simple written and oral texts (emails, ads, short stories)
will be able to localise themselves, describe places and give instruction
will be able to express preferences, needs, and abilities
will gain awareness of Italian culture and daily life

courses.name: Italian IV
courses.purpose: The students will learn to communicate in everyday situations, use basic grammar structures, understand and produce short written and oral texts, build vocabulary on key topics, gain insight into Italian culture and daily life, and participate in simple conversations and group activities.

learning_outcomes: Students will be able to understand and produce simple oral and written texts related to familiar contexts (shopping, daily life, social activities, work).
Students will be able to apply basic grammatical structures (e.g. imperative, future, conditional, comparatives) in both spoken and written communication.
Students will be able to use vocabulary related to clothing, travel, emotions, jobs, and social interaction appropriately.
Students will be able to interact in short conversations and role plays using functional expressions (asking for information, giving advice, expressing opinions).
Students will be able to understand short authentic materials (e.g. job ads, invitations, horoscopes, CVs) and respond accordingly.
Students will be able to demonstrate awareness of Italian cultural norms in everyday situations.

courses.name: Formations of Modernity
courses.purpose: This course provides a descriptive, historical account of the key political, social, economic and cultural processes that shaped modernity. It also aims to analyze the key concepts, issues and current debates in the related academic literature and to present an overall picture of modern times.
courses.description: What is modernity? Conceptualizing the experience of modernity; discussions around the concept of modernity; the Renaissance and the reformation movement; the process of colonization; the Enlightenment; the French Revolution and the Industrial Revolution; the 19th century development of European metropolis; the emergence of modern subject; modernity and the formation of everyday life; class, gender and modernity; modernity, utopia and dystopia; modernity and the avant-garde; late modernity and globalization; overview of the semester and discussion of different perceptions of modernity.
learning_outcomes: have a clear conception of modern society and its institutions
develop the ability to think theoretically and conceptually
develop the ability to communicate and discuss issues effectively
develop the ability to read and understand texts, make research and write
develop the capacity to locate and evaluate engineering problems in their social contexts

courses.name: Topics in Hist.and Society

courses.description: This course is an introduction to historical sociology, which focuses on the intersection of history and sociology. It examines the logic and methodological assumptions of historical sociology as a field of analysis through a survey of representative works and developments within the evolution of the field. Substantive paradigms of comparative and historical analysis are also illustrated through a discussion of major works, such as those of Michael Mann, Norbert Elias, Charles Tilly, Shmuel Eisenstadt, Theda Skocpol, Fernand Braudel, Immanuel Wallerstein, Marc Bloch and Perry Anderson.
learning_outcomes: Be able to establish the basis of a notion of history and to understand how the fates of modern world societies were determined and developed through the processes of constant material interchange

Read and understand texts, make research and write

courses.name: Environment and Society
courses.purpose: To introduce historical and contemporary discussions on environmental issues and the environment concept within the social sciences framework. The course also aims to develop theoretical and conceptual thinking, communication, reading, research, and writing skills, and to help students evaluate engineering problems in their social contexts.
courses.description: Environment, human systems and social sciences; the Anthropocene; ecological crisis and the social constitution of the environment from a historical perspective; the concept of environment in social theory; political responses such as limiting growth and managing the environment; sustainability, resilience, and radical environmentalism; systemic causes of environmental disruption including globalization, materialism, consumption, economy, technology, population, and development; social consequences such as environmental justice; climate change mitigation, adaptation, system change, and geoengineering; environmental movement and environmental governance.
learning_outcomes: Understand and evaluate environment concept and environmental issues within social sciences perspective and relate this appreciation to historical and contemporary context
Think theoretically and conceptually
Communicate and discuss issues effectively
Read and understand texts, make research and write
Locate and evaluate engineering problems in their social contexts

courses.name: Anthropology
courses.purpose: Guiding students in seeing the world from an anthropological perspective. Developing ability to think and read critically; developing an ability of creative thinking and writing; developing ability to ask strategic questions; developing the ability to think theoretically and conceptually; developing the ability to communicate and discuss issues effectively; developing the ability to read and understand texts, make research and write.
courses.description: This course covers general topics of interest in the discipline of anthropology. The major theories and methods of anthropology will be used to account for the similarities and diversities in human cultures in a comparative perspective. The course will be an introduction to the holistic approaches that evaluate the meanings of and interactions between diverse cultural phenomena, such as environment, ecology, language, belief systems, symbolic systems and meanings, myths, family, marriage and kinship, gender, enculturation processes, political organizations, and economic practices. Through readings, lectures, films and discussions the themes, conceptual frameworks and techniques of research in anthropology will be learned.
learning_outcomes: Students who succeed in this course will improve their ability to analyze the socio-cultural and environmental processes.
Students who succeed in this course will improve their ability to learn the methods of cross-cultural comparison.
Students who succeed in this course will improve their ability to learn the techniques of cross-cultural communication and cultural analysis.
Students who succeed in this course will improve their ability to learn conceptual frameworks and methods in the field of anthropology.
Students who succeed in this course will improve their ability to gain an ability to conduct cultural analysis through the concept of cultural relativism.
Students who succeed in this course will improve their ability to read and understand texts, conduct research and develop writing skills.
resources: Hasty, J, D. Lewis and M. Snipes. 2022. Introduction to Anthropology. Opensax. (electronic book) (https://openstax.org/details/books/introduction-anthropology)
Brown et al. 2023. Perspectives: An Introduction to Cultural Anthropology. (https://pressbooks.pub/perspectives/)

courses.name: Economics
courses.purpose: To identify key basic concepts in both micro and macroeconomics. To demonstrate how basic micro and macroeconomic models work. To understand how consumers and firms make their decisions and how government intervenes. To motivate the use of basic economic concepts to analyze contemporary macro problems.
courses.description: This course examines the basic concepts in economic science. The first part covers microeconomics, which focuses on how firms decide on production and households decide on the consumption of goods and services. The second part covers macroeconomics that studies the economy as a whole. Topics include national output, unemployment, inflation, economic growth, and monetary and fiscal policies.
learning_outcomes: Competency to use microeconomic concepts such as tradeoff, opportunity cost, elasticity, and gains from trade.
Competency to use macroeconomic concepts such as interest rate, unemployment, inflation, economic growth, and monetary and fiscal policies.
Competency to explain the rationale for government intervention into micro and macroeconomic problems.
Competency to analyze and to explain economic and social problems faced in our everyday life.

courses.purpose: Design and implement a system, component, or process to meet desired needs. Formulate and solve engineering problems. Use the techniques, skills, and modern engineering tools necessary for engineering practice. Communicate effectively.
courses.description: This non-lecture course contains a design project. Two or three people groups can be formed for the project. The design project which can be a software or a hardware project should contain all the phases starting with project analysis to project completion by giving a presentation. The course leads the students to use the knowledge they gained during their education in the department.
learning_outcomes: An ability to design and implement a system, component, or process to meet desired needs.
An ability to formulate, and solve engineering problems.
An ability to use the techniques, skills, and modern engineering tools necessary for engineering practice.
An ability to communicate effectively.
An ability to produce a detailed project report
